# tuna

> Cloudflare Tunnels for humans. Wrap any dev command with a secure, persistent tunnel.

## What is tuna?

tuna is an open-source CLI that wraps your development server commands with automatic Cloudflare Tunnel setup. Instead of manually creating tunnels, configuring DNS, and managing credentials, you just prefix your command with `tuna`.

- Website: https://tuna.oxc.sh
- Docs: https://docs.tuna.oxc.sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/zeroexcore/tuna
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroexcore/tuna

## Install

```bash
npm install -g @zeroexcore/tuna
```

## Setup

```bash
tuna --login   # Opens browser, creates API token with right permissions
tuna --init    # Interactive project setup
```

## Usage

```bash
tuna vite dev
tuna npm run dev
tuna next dev
```

This starts your dev server AND creates a Cloudflare Tunnel, making it available at your configured domain (e.g. `https://my-app.example.com`).

## Configuration

Add to `package.json`:

```json
{
  "tuna": {
    "forward": "my-app.example.com",
    "port": 3000
  }
}
```

### Config fields

- `forward` (string, required): Domain to expose. Supports `$USER` and `$TUNA_USER` for per-developer subdomains.
- `port` (number, required): Local port to forward (1-65535).
- `access` (string[], optional): Email addresses or `@domain` patterns for Zero Trust access control.

### Team collaboration

```json
{
  "tuna": {
    "forward": "$USER-api.example.com",
    "port": 3000
  }
}
```

Each developer gets their own subdomain automatically.

### Access control

```json
{
  "tuna": {
    "forward": "staging.example.com",
    "port": 3000,
    "access": ["@mycompany.com", "contractor@gmail.com"]
  }
}
```

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `tuna <command>` | Wrap command with tunnel |
| `tuna --init` | Interactive project setup |
| `tuna --login` | Setup Cloudflare credentials |
| `tuna --list` | List all tunnels |
| `tuna --stop` | Stop cloudflared service |
| `tuna --delete [name]` | Delete tunnel |

## Requirements

- Node.js 18+
- Cloudflare account with a domain
- macOS (Linux/Windows support planned)

## How it works

tuna uses cloudflared under the hood. It manages tunnel creation, DNS records, ingress config, and credentials automatically. Tunnels run as a launchd service on macOS, surviving terminal restarts.

## Full documentation

For complete docs see: https://docs.tuna.oxc.sh
For full LLM context see: https://tuna.oxc.sh/llms-full.txt
